text: The eProcurement system shall support the procurement cycle from catalogue through to remittance.
All electronic documents shall be marked-up using eXtensible Markup Language (XML) and validated using XML schema, in compliance with the e-Government Interoperability Framework (e-GIF).
All documents shall include a mandatory status attribute with values of 'original' 'copy' 'revision' or 'cancellation'.
The system shall provide a Catalogue document from the Seller to the Buyer to provide Item price and other information.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Request for Quotation (RFQ) to the Seller to request supply terms.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a Quotation to the Buyer to set out terms and conditions for supplying goods or services.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Purchase Order to the Seller to communicate the intent to purchase.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a PO Response to the Buyer to acknowledge receipt and intent to fulfill a Purchase Order.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a Fulfilment Notification to the Buyer to inform that goods have been despatched or delivered.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Receipt Advice to the Seller to acknowledge receipt and describe discovered variances.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a Rectification Advice to the Buyer to inform of action regarding goods or services at variance.
The system shall enable the Seller to send an Invoice to the Buyer to request payment for goods or services.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Self Billed Invoice to the Seller for self-billing arrangements.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a Credit Note to the Buyer to confirm that the Seller owes money.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Debit Note to the Seller to state that the Buyer has deducted amounts from an Invoice.
The system shall enable the Buyer to send a Remittance Advice to the Seller to notify payment and state accounted documents.
The system shall enable the Seller to send a Statement to the Buyer summarizing the status of the Buyer's account.
The system shall assign a mandatory Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) to every Document instance.
The system shall assign a mandatory Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) to every Line instance.
Every Document shall include a mandatory Document ID assigned by the sender's system.
Every Document shall include a mandatory Document Date using the format CCYYMMDDhhmmss.
Every Document shall include a mandatory Test Status boolean attribute.
Every Document shall include the mandatory URI of the Sender Software Manufacturer.
Every Document shall include the mandatory version of the XML schema with which it complies.
Every Document Line shall include a mandatory Line Number starting with 1 up to a maximum of 9999.
Every Document Line shall include a mandatory Line Status attribute with values 'original' 'revision' or 'deletion'.
Every Item shall include a mandatory Item Name limited to 70 characters.
Every Item shall include a mandatory Unit of Measure code based on UN Recommendation No. 20.
Every Priced Item shall include a mandatory Unit Price with a 3-character ISO 4217 currency code.
Every Priced Item shall include a mandatory Commodity Class using UNSPSC or an OGC-approved classification.
The system shall support a Digital Signature as the final element of the XML payload if the sender system is capable.
